Package neqsim.statistics.parameterfitting

Class ExperimentalDataSet

java.lang.Object
neqsim.statistics.parameterfitting.ExperimentalDataSet
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class ExperimentalDataSet extends Object implements Serializable
Metadata-aware collection of experimental data points for parameter fitting.
Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • ExperimentalDataSet

      public ExperimentalDataSet(String name, String responseName, String responseUnit, String[] dependentVariableNames, String[] dependentVariableUnits)
      Creates an experimental data set.
      Parameters:
      name - data set name
      responseName - measured response name
      responseUnit - measured response unit
      dependentVariableNames - names of independent variables passed to the fitting function
      dependentVariableUnits - units of independent variables passed to the fitting function
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dependent variable metadata is empty or inconsistent

    • ExperimentalDataSet

      public ExperimentalDataSet(String name, String responseName, String[] dependentVariableNames)
      Creates an experimental data set without explicit unit metadata.
      Parameters:
      name - data set name
      responseName - measured response name
      dependentVariableNames - names of independent variables passed to the fitting function
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dependent variable metadata is empty

  • Method Details

    • addPoint

      public ExperimentalDataSet addPoint(ExperimentalDataPoint point)
      Adds an experimental data point.
      Parameters:
      point - data point to add
      Returns:
      this data set for fluent construction
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if point is null or has the wrong number of independent values

    • addPoint

      public ExperimentalDataSet addPoint(double measuredValue, double standardDeviation, double[] dependentValues)
      Adds an experimental data point.
      Parameters:
      measuredValue - measured response value
      standardDeviation - positive standard deviation for the measured response
      dependentValues - independent variable values used by the fitting function
      Returns:
      this data set for fluent construction
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if values are invalid or dimensions do not match metadata

    • addPoint

      public ExperimentalDataSet addPoint(double measuredValue, double standardDeviation, double[] dependentValues, String reference, String description)
      Adds an experimental data point with reference metadata.
      Parameters:
      measuredValue - measured response value
      standardDeviation - positive standard deviation for the measured response
      dependentValues - independent variable values used by the fitting function
      reference - source reference for the data point
      description - short description of the data point
      Returns:
      this data set for fluent construction
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if values are invalid or dimensions do not match metadata

    • toSampleSet

      public SampleSet toSampleSet(BaseFunction function)
      Converts the data set to the legacy SampleSet representation.
      Parameters:
      function - fitting function to attach to each sample
      Returns:
      sample set compatible with the existing optimizer API
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if function is null

    • split

      public ExperimentalDataSet[] split(double trainingFraction)
      Splits the data set into training and validation subsets while preserving point order.
      Parameters:
      trainingFraction - fraction of rows assigned to the training subset, exclusive range 0.0 < trainingFraction < 1.0
      Returns:
      two data sets: index 0 is training and index 1 is validation
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the fraction is outside the valid range or fewer than two points are available

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the data set name.
      Returns:
      data set name

    • getResponseName

      public String getResponseName()
      Returns the response name.
      Returns:
      measured response name

    • getResponseUnit

      public String getResponseUnit()
      Returns the response unit.
      Returns:
      measured response unit

    • getDependentVariableNames

      public String[] getDependentVariableNames()
      Returns independent variable names.
      Returns:
      copy of independent variable names

    • getDependentVariableUnits

      public String[] getDependentVariableUnits()
      Returns independent variable units.
      Returns:
      copy of independent variable units

    • getDependentVariableCount

      public int getDependentVariableCount()
      Returns the number of independent variables.
      Returns:
      independent variable count

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of data points.
      Returns:
      point count

    • getPoint

      public ExperimentalDataPoint getPoint(int index)
      Returns one data point.
      Parameters:
      index - zero-based point index
      Returns:
      experimental data point
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is outside the point list

    • getPoints

      public List<ExperimentalDataPoint> getPoints()
      Returns an immutable snapshot of the data points.
      Returns:
      immutable point list snapshot

    • fromCsv

      public static ExperimentalDataSet fromCsv(File file, String name, String responseName, String responseUnit, String[] dependentVariableNames, String[] dependentVariableUnits) throws IOException
      Reads an experimental data set from a CSV file.
      Parameters:
      file - CSV file with a header row
      name - data set name
      responseName - response name and default measured-value column
      responseUnit - response unit used in the resulting data set
      dependentVariableNames - independent variable names and default column names
      dependentVariableUnits - independent variable units used in the resulting data set
      Returns:
      experimental data set
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be read

    • fromCsv

      public static ExperimentalDataSet fromCsv(File file, ExperimentalDataReader.CsvOptions options) throws IOException
      Reads an experimental data set from a CSV file using explicit mapping options.
      Parameters:
      file - CSV file with a header row
      options - CSV mapping options
      Returns:
      experimental data set
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be read

    • fromJson

      public static ExperimentalDataSet fromJson(String json) throws IOException
      Reads an experimental data set from JSON text.
      Parameters:
      json - JSON text
      Returns:
      experimental data set
      Throws:
      IOException - if parsing fails

    • fromJson

      public static ExperimentalDataSet fromJson(File file) throws IOException
      Reads an experimental data set from a JSON file.
      Parameters:
      file - JSON file
      Returns:
      experimental data set
      Throws:
      IOException - if reading or parsing fails

    • fromYaml

      public static ExperimentalDataSet fromYaml(String yaml) throws IOException
      Reads an experimental data set from YAML text.
      Parameters:
      yaml - YAML text
      Returns:
      experimental data set
      Throws:
      IOException - if parsing fails

    • fromYaml

      public static ExperimentalDataSet fromYaml(File file) throws IOException
      Reads an experimental data set from a YAML file.
      Parameters:
      file - YAML file
      Returns:
      experimental data set
      Throws:
      IOException - if reading or parsing fails

    • createEmptyCopy

      private ExperimentalDataSet createEmptyCopy(String newName)
      Creates an empty data set with the same metadata.
      Parameters:
      newName - name for the copied data set
      Returns:
      empty data set with copied metadata

    • validateDependentVariableMetadata

      private static void validateDependentVariableMetadata(String[] names, String[] units)
      Validates dependent variable metadata.
      Parameters:
      names - dependent variable names
      units - dependent variable units
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if metadata is empty or inconsistent

    • createEmptyUnits

      private static String[] createEmptyUnits(String[] dependentVariableNames)
      Creates empty unit labels for each dependent variable.
      Parameters:
      dependentVariableNames - dependent variable names
      Returns:
      empty unit labels
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dependentVariableNames is null

    • defaultString

      private static String defaultString(String value, String defaultValue)
      Returns a default string if the supplied value is null.
      Parameters:
      value - user supplied value
      defaultValue - fallback value
      Returns:
      value or defaultValue if value is null

    • copyArray

      private static String[] copyArray(String[] values)
      Copies a string array.
      Parameters:
      values - values to copy
      Returns:
      copied string array
